One can learn so much in a few hours at these events. It exposes you to the technology in real world and tells you what’s happening on ground. 

What are the top products right now❓
What kind of ideas are getting funded by Y Combinator❓

Not just this,

author.name: Sukin Shetty
author.description: AI Architect | AI Product Builder | Founder @ Solution Forge Labs | Creator of Nemp Memory | Building GhostOps. Helping Businesses build Real AI Systems .

text:
I had participated #vibecon beautifully organized by @nitprashant @emergentlabs team. It was an amazing experience. It is not easy to organize such a grand event. If you weren’t part of it, don’t worry you will get an opportunity. I saw some amazing products and met builders who were deeply passionate about what they were creating. With 250+ products being showcased in a short time, judging is never easy. That only reminded me how much effort goes into building something truly meaningful.

But the experience was mindblowing kudos to @emergentlabs team to finish this event at global level. They showed how a world class, global level hackathon should be organized. It was not hackathon, it was a festival. Hats off to you guys. 
My advice don’t go to hackathon to win, got to build something which you were waiting for very long time. And to meet like minded people. You will learn a lot from them.

text:
Mukesh Ambani said it best.
“Content is King. Distribution is GOD.”

@emergentlabs | @polaris_code | @ycombinator | @nitprashant 

Let that sink in. It doesn’t matter how great your product is, how brilliant your content is, or how big your idea is. If distribution is broken, none of it reaches the right person. Ever.
Right now India’s creator economy is growing like nothing else. Every brand, every startup, every creator is producing. But here’s the truth nobody is talking about. We are not going to have a content problem in India. We are going to have a distribution problem.
Brands spend crores on creation. Zero infrastructure exists to pay for distribution at scale. No verified system. No automation. No same-day payouts. Nothing.

That is the single biggest unsolved problem in India’s attention economy. And that is exactly what 17Katas is building.

Y Combinator (YC), one of the world’s most influential startup accelerators, has partnered with Polaris School of Technology and Emergent to introduce a new pathway for early-stage student talent in India to enter the YC ecosystem.

The partnership introduces the Vibecon Student Track, a hackathon designed to identify high-agency student builders and give them a direct opportunity to engage with YC through a live, on-ground build and evaluation process.

Students from across India apply in teams of 2-3 by sharing short pitches on social platforms that detail the problem they are solving and their build plan on the Emergent platform.

Out of thousands of applicants, only the top 5 teams will be shortlisted for the on-ground finals at the Polaris campus in Bengaluru on April 16-17.

text:
If you’re building in AI right now, you’ll want to read this👇 

I’ve received an exclusive invite to attend OpenAI Engineers Day on Jan 31 at Polaris School of Technology and this is shaping up to be one of the most builder-focused AI gatherings in India.

This isn’t a “sit in an audience and listen to keynotes” kind of event. It’s designed for engineers, developers, and technical founders who are actively building real-world AI systems. The vibe is hands-on, deeply technical, and very community-driven.

If you’re someone working seriously in AI this year, whether that’s infrastructure, applications, research, or product, this might be worth checking out.

Here’s the link with details:
🔗 https://t.co/f7FEUvadA3

If you end up going, let me know, always nice to meet fellow builders offline 🙂

Rooms like this are where real collaborations start. If you’re serious about building in AI, this is the kind of space you want to be in.

text: The billboards' claims about Google Summer of Code and Summer of Bitcoin stipends seem exaggerated. GSoC 2025 stipends for India are around $3,000 USD (₹2.56 lakh), not ₹12 lakh or ₹7.5 lakh as claimed. Summer of Bitcoin's past stipends were about $3,000 USD (₹2.56 lakh), aligning with the ₹2.5 lakh claim but not higher amounts. Polaris School of Technology exists, but no independent evidence confirms these specific achievements. The claims likely inflate stipends for marketing. Always verify such bold claims with official sources.
